dashboard.label: T1071.004 — DNS C2 (dnscat2)
dashboard.description: DNS tunneling C2 detection. Aggregates Zeek dns.log; flags high-volume, long-subdomain, high-cardinality queries to a single base domain.

search.query: `dns_tunnel_dnscat2` | where like(src, "$src_filter$") OR "$src_filter$"="*" | eval window=strftime(_time, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M") | sort -query_count | table window, src, base_domain, query_count, unique_queries, avg_sub_len, avg_uniq_chars, qtypes, sample_subdomains

panel.title: Raw query volume — all DNS by source/domain

search.query: index=zeek sourcetype=zeek:dns | where like(src, "$src_filter$") OR "$src_filter$"="*" | eval _parts=split(query, ".") | eval n=mvcount(_parts) | where n>=2 | eval base_domain=mvindex(_parts, n-2) . "." . mvindex(_parts, n-1) | timechart span=5m count BY base_domain limit=8

html.p: dnscat2 establishes C2 by tunnelling encoded payloads inside DNS queries to an attacker-controlled domain. The implant prefers TXT records (largest response payload) and packs each subdomain near the 63-byte label limit.

The detection groups Zeek dns.log records by (5-min bucket, src, base domain) and requires all of: query volume ≥ 50, average subdomain length ≥ 20, average unique-char count ≥ 12, distinct queries ≥ 30, and at least one TXT/MX/CNAME/NULL query.
